content.body: This document exists to define the shared JSON wrapper around App Behavior spec collections. Use it when creating collection files, manifests, generated review inputs, or import/export payloads.

content.title: Purpose
body: The artifact envelope gives every UX JSON file predictable metadata without forcing every file to contain the same item type. It allows scripts and DB importers to answer:
- which UX modes are represented by the artifact
- which artifact role the file serves
- what kind of artifact the file contains
- where generated Markdown should be written
- whether items are portable and stable

content.title: Required Envelope Fields
body: - `artifactRole`: object or artifact kind, such as `requirement`, `mapping`, or `mixed`
- `uxModes`: product behavior modes represented by the artifact, such as `current`, `desired`, or `intermediate`

content.title: Governance Rule
body: Every production UX artifact should make this explicit:
Markdown review files may summarize or format UX data, but the JSON envelope and items remain the source of truth.

content.title: Split And Compose Rule
content.body: Envelope-level metadata may help scripts split or compose files, but item meaning must not depend on file location alone. Every item should carry enough `id`, `mode`, `owner`, `scope`, and status data to remain meaningful after being moved.
